harness_lifecycle.note: fail-fast race between `Copy report` and `Retry`; determines completion_state
produces {scope, strategy, candidateCount, qualifiedCount, selectedLength, pageBodyCharCount, fallbackUsed, ...}
invokes scripts/quote-integrity-check.mjs via spawnSync; writes quote_integrity_summary.json under runDir
sections: Red / Amber / Quote integrity / Network diagnostics / Artifacts
legacy exit code from classify(checks) · **not affected by QI or structural**

cons: would need structural before QI to write to metadata block ordering — but ordering is easy either way
invocation_point_options.recommendation: consider only if we want structural to be first-class alongside QI

pros: matches QI symmetry — both after report.md save · both write JSON to runDir
QI already ran so no race
cons: cleanest but requires small helper for spawn
capture context still fine but symmetry with QI weaker
harder to include in metadata block if we want structural.summary_path referenced there
verdict.md would not include structural block
two-write pattern (verdict.md then patch) is fragile

selected_invocation_point.location_hint: immediately after line ~828 `runQuoteIntegrity(...)` returns and before line ~830 first `checks.push`
selected_invocation_point.rationale: symmetric with QI · report.md and capture context both final · metadata block can include both `quote_integrity` and `structural_evidence` sub-blocks · verdict.md can display both side-by-side · minimal disturbance to legacy 25-check flow · legacy exit code produced by classify() remains untouched
constraints: must not spawn if report.md was not saved (mirror QI's blocked_no_report path)
must never rethrow; helper returns an envelope like `runQuoteIntegrity` does
must not push into the `checks[]` array (legacy verdict source-of-truth stays untouched)

cons: opaque · poor schema · leaks across processes
harder to inspect · CLI shell composition awkward · debuggability

selected_context_contract.kind: temporary JSON file · single new CLI flag `--context <path>`
selected_context_contract.path_convention: harness writes to `<runDir>/structural_evidence_context.json` (retained as a sanitized artifact for audit; small file · minimal fields)

fields.capture_scope: one of: main section | body | none · matches harness capture.scope
fields.completion_state: one of: success | application_error | hard_timeout | navigation_error | not_started
fields.capture_complete: boolean · true only when completion_state=success AND capture_scope covers full report (main section captured OR body includes both gap section and Appendix)
fields.report_capture_error: string | null · non-null when reportText was empty or extraction failed
fields.expected_sections_captured: boolean · true when required section markers (Target role, Your top 5 gaps, Evidence Appendix) are present in reportText

selected_context_contract.artifact_persistence: retain sanitized context file in runDir for audit; do NOT embed full report body or long quote excerpts

cli_extension_design.selected: extend structural-evidence-check.mjs with OPTIONAL `--context <path>` while preserving current two-argument behavior
cli_extension_design.backward_compat:
existing Phase 1 invocation `--report

--output

` continues to work · synthetic marker `` remains supported for Phase 1 test compatibility
cli_extension_design.precedence: explicit context takes precedence over synthetic marker · real harness never emits or depends on the marker
cli_extension_design.context_missing_but_flag_absent: preserve standalone behavior (no context = pure text-based verdict)
cli_extension_design.context_schema_version_unknown: tool_error exit 2 · do NOT silently ignore

capture_sufficiency_rules.fallback_used_alone_note: fallback_used=true does NOT automatically imply not_evaluable · if fallback still captures the full report (expected_sections_captured=true), classification proceeds normally; the AMBER-level `report_capture_scope_not_body` legacy check continues to surface the fallback visibility separately
capture_sufficiency_rules.unknown_capture_scope: must NOT silently become evaluable · treat as not_evaluable with reason `unknown_capture_scope`

artifact_behavior.artifact_atomicity: validator writes via .tmp-<pid> + renameSync (already in Phase 1)
artifact_behavior.temp_context_persistence: retain sanitized context file — small, aids audit, no proprietary content

metadata_integration.tool_error_behavior: evaluation_status=tool_error · verdict=null · exit_code recorded (2 or null on spawn fail) · summary_path=null unless partial artifact exists (should NOT unless validator misbehaves)
metadata_integration.not_run_behavior: evaluation_status=not_run · verdict=null · used when reportText not saved (blocked_no_report analogue)
metadata_integration.single_metadata_write: harness patches structural_evidence block INTO the metadata object built at line ~1080 BEFORE writing metadata.json (single atomic write)

required_wording: Structural telemetry does NOT affect legacy verdict.
Quote-integrity telemetry does NOT affect legacy verdict.
RED telemetry is not silently presented as overall GREEN.
Overall display must not mask either result.

combined_telemetry.location: metadata.combined_telemetry (single source of truth) · echoed in verdict.md

rules.red_if: either QI or structural verdict === red
rules.amber_if: neither red AND (QI or structural verdict === amber)
rules.not_evaluable_if: one cannot be evaluated AND neither is red

combined_telemetry.phase_2_impact: does NOT affect harness process exit code · does NOT affect legacy verdict · display-only

validator_exit_0.harness_action: continue normally · record in metadata + verdict.md

validator_exit_1.harness_action: continue normally · record RED in structural telemetry · legacy verdict unchanged · legacy process exit unchanged

validator_exit_2.action: record tool_error · continue metadata/verdict.md write · legacy verdict unchanged · do NOT retry · do NOT silently ignore

exit_handling.spawn_fail: record tool_error with reason `validator_spawn_failed` · exit_code=null · duration_ms=elapsed until failure
exit_handling.artifact_missing_despite_exit_0_or_1: record tool_error with reason `artifact_missing_after_exit_${code}` · verdict=null · legacy verdict unchanged
exit_handling.checker_hash_compute_fail: record tool_error with reason `checker_hash_compute_failed` · continue with verdict from artifact if present
exit_handling.context_file_write_fail: record tool_error with reason `context_write_failed` · skip validator invocation · continue metadata/verdict.md write

timeout_policy.rationale: validator is local · linear scan of a Markdown report · Phase 1 tests complete in <1 s each · 5 s provides ample headroom
timeout_policy.timeout_effect: kill child process · record tool_error with reason `validator_timeout` · no retry · legacy verdict unchanged · legacy latency thresholds (HARD_LATENCY_MS=240_000 · SOFT_LATENCY_MS=120_000) unchanged

implementation_hash.rationale: identifies exact validator implementation independent of git state · robust against local modifications · comparable across environments · rejected: short git commit (requires git binary access + repo state) · git blob hash (harder to reproduce)

test_matrix.expected: metadata.structural_evidence.verdict=red · legacy verdict=green · legacy exit unchanged
verdict=not_evaluable · legacy verdict unchanged · reason present
not_evaluable with reason from harness context
verdict from report content · GREEN if healthy

test_matrix.scenario: no baseline files changed by harness invocation
no report body embedded in metadata/verdict/context
test_matrix.expected: grep across artifacts finds no >120-char verbatim substring of report
single spawn call per run · no second attempt
exit code matches classify(checks).exit regardless of structural verdict

cons: increased risk to legacy verdict path

selected_test_architecture: B_new_helper_module (integration adapter) + supplemental static source assertions for backward-compatibility guarantees

changed_files_expected: scripts/structural-evidence-check.mjs (extend with `--context` support only · preserve all existing behavior · +~40 lines)
scripts/test-structural-evidence-check.mjs (add context-mode tests · preserve all 26 existing tests · +~150 lines)
scripts/report-regression-local.mjs (invoke validator + metadata block + verdict.md block · +~80 lines)

phase_2_rollback_steps: structural-evidence-check.mjs --context extension can remain (backward-compatible) OR be reverted
remove metadata.structural_evidence block emission from harness
remove `## Structural evidence telemetry` section from verdict.md
no prompt rollback required (Phase 2 does not touch prompt)
no baseline restoration required (Phase 2 does not mutate baselines)

Q1_integration_point.answer: Option B — immediately after runQuoteIntegrity() and before first legacy checks.push

Q2_cli_args_or_context_json.answer: Option B — temporary JSON context file via optional `--context <path>`

Q3_context_overrides_marker.answer: YES — explicit context takes precedence over synthetic marker; real harness never emits marker

Q5_fallback_used_implies_not_evaluable.answer: NO — fallback_used=true alone does not imply not_evaluable; must also have expected_sections_captured=false

Q6_exit_1_behavior.answer: record structural verdict=RED · include in metadata + verdict.md · legacy verdict unchanged · legacy process exit unchanged

Q7_exit_2_behavior.answer: record tool_error · include in metadata + verdict.md · legacy verdict unchanged · no retry · no silent ignore

Q10_combined_telemetry_storage.answer: metadata.combined_telemetry (single source of truth) + brief echo in verdict.md

Q11_validator_hash.answer: SHA-256 content hash of scripts/structural-evidence-check.mjs, stored as `checker_hash: "sha256:<hex>"`

Q16_phase_3_auto_authorized.answer: NO — Phase 3 (paid A/B) requires separate DECISION + human GO + ~$0.10 cost approval

Q19_incomplete_capture_classification.answer: not_evaluable — never RED; classified via explicit harness context, not by report-text inference

risks: Adding a helper module + harness patch is the largest scripts/ change since 5c-integrate; regression risk mitigated by keeping structural block strictly additive and by covering it with deterministic integration tests

Metadata schema addition may affect downstream consumers if any exist (currently none — 5e-migrate proved zero code consumers of baseline_metadata.json; run-level metadata.json has no known consumers either)
verdict.md format change is user-facing (for human review); wording must not imply structural affects legacy verdict
SHA-256 hash requires readFileSync on every run — cheap but adds a tiny bit of I/O
Context JSON file adds one write per run; retained for audit but may confuse users unfamiliar with its purpose (mitigated by docstring/comment)
Timeout of 5 s must not be triggered by legitimate reports; validator is fast but future report growth could exceed if implementation is refactored
Combined telemetry field must be clearly labeled as non-blocking to avoid misinterpretation
Backward compat with Phase 1 CLI (no --context) must remain — validator MUST NOT require --context
If harness spawn logic differs subtly between platforms (macOS/Linux), integration helper module must handle both

Not-evaluable classification depends on harness context correctness; a bug in harness context construction could hide real Appendix omissions — mitigated by cross-checking capture_scope enumeration explicitly
